title: DSDS Guide Definitions
description: Defines documents for long-form, reading-oriented guidance that is not tied to a single component, token, foundation, or pattern. A guide covers getting-started walkthroughs, contribution instructions, tutorials, conceptual overviews, migration guides, and any other narrative docs a design system needs. A guide document holds identity and metadata. The `category` metadata field sets the kind of guide (getting-started, contribution, tutorial, and so on). This keeps the entity flexible enough for any guide. The `metadata` object holds optional fields. The `documentBlocks` array holds structured docs as typed entries: narrative sections, procedures, guidelines, and use-cases.

guide.description: A long-form, reading-oriented document: a getting-started walkthrough, a contribution guide, a tutorial, a conceptual overview, a migration guide, or any narrative guidance the system needs. Guides fill the gap left by the artifact-bound entities (component, token, foundation, pattern). Those entities document a single thing, not a journey or a concept. The top level holds its identity: `identifier`, `name`, and `description`. The `metadata` object holds optional fields. Use `category` (e.g., 'getting-started', 'contribution', 'tutorial', 'concept', 'migration') to classify the guide, and `tags` for finer-grained discovery. The `documentBlocks` array holds structured docs as typed entries: sections (narrative prose), steps (procedures), guidelines, use-cases, imports, accessibility, and content.

kind.description: Identifies this entity as a guide.

identifier.description: Machine-readable identifier for the guide (e.g., 'getting-started', 'contributing', 'theming-tutorial', 'migrating-to-v2'). MUST be lowercase kebab-case and unique within its entity group.

name.description: Display name shown in docs (e.g., 'Getting started', 'Contributing', 'Theming tutorial', 'Migrating to v2').

description.description: What this guide covers, who it is for, and what the reader will accomplish. CommonMark supported.

metadata.description: Optional metadata: the shared entityMetadata fields (see metadata/metadata.schema.json). Use `category` to classify the guide (e.g., 'getting-started', 'contribution', 'tutorial', 'concept', 'migration').

relationships.description: Typed dependency edges from this guide to the entities it documents or depends on. Inverse edges are derived by tooling. Use the `links` metadata field for external resources.

documentBlocks.description: All structured docs for this guide, as an ordered array of typed document block objects. It accepts the guide-specific kind (steps), the reused imports kind (setup instructions), plus the general kinds (guidelines, use-cases, accessibility, content, sections, checklist). The free-form sections block is a guide's backbone. The `kind` property on each entry sets its shape. A guide reads top to bottom, so order matters and tools SHOULD keep it for display. Put links to external resources in the `links` metadata field or a section's `links`, not in document block entries; point at other entities through the `relationships` array.

agentDocumentBlocks.description: Structured docs for agents (AI/LLM) only — a separate space that accepts the same block kinds as `documentBlocks`. `documentBlocks` is the default: put anything a human reader needs there. Use `agentDocumentBlocks` only for content that helps an agent build correctly but is noise for humans. For example: hard MUST/MUST-NOT rules an agent should treat as firm limits; notes that correct a mistake an agent is likely to make, such as reaching for this entity when a confusable one fits better; constraints backed by test-run evidence; and machine-checkable criteria with verification modes. A hard rule that humans also need (such as an accessibility requirement) belongs in `documentBlocks`, not here. Agent blocks add to the human docs — they never replace or duplicate them. Tools MUST NOT render these blocks in human-facing docs. Agents SHOULD read both arrays, human blocks first.
